perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the parties under this Agreement will expire three (3) years from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the parties under this Agreement related to confidentiality will expire three (3) years from the date of this Agreement. All other obligations will survive the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by specifying that only confidentiality obligations expire after three years, while 'all other obligations' survive termination. This creates a contradiction with paragraph (3), which requires the return or destruction of Evaluation Material if a transaction is not consummated or if either party requests it. If 'all other obligations' survive, then the obligation to return or destroy Evaluation Material persists even after three years, conflicting with the three-year limit on confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If a transaction between the Companies is not consummated or if either party at any time so requests, each party will promptly return to the other party all copies of the Evaluation Material in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives, and each party will destroy all copies of any analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by it or for its use containing or reflecting any Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all obligations expire after three years. The changed text specifies that only confidentiality obligations expire after three years, and all other obligations survive termination. The contradicted text requires the return or destruction of Evaluation Material under certain conditions. Because 'all other obligations' survive termination, including the obligation to return or destroy evaluation material, even after the three-year limit on confidentiality. Thus, the modified text contradicts the original agreement, creating an inconsistency about what obligations end after three years, satisfying the definition of inconsistencies - in text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that the other party is making no representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material, and neither the disclosing party, nor any of its respective officers, directors, employees, stockholders, affiliates or agents will have any liability to the other party or any other person resulting from such other party's use of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that the other party is making no representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy of the Evaluation Material, and neither the disclosing party, nor any of its respective officers, directors, employees, stockholders, affiliates or agents will have any liability to the other party or any other person resulting from such other party's use of the Evaluation Material. However, this does not apply to any intentionally misleading information.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a contradiction by adding 'However, this does not apply to any intentionally misleading information' to the disclaimer of warranties. Initially, the agreement states that no party is liable for the other party's use of the Evaluation Material. By adding this exception, the text implies that liability may arise if intentionally misleading information is provided. This contradicts the initial statement of non-liability and could create conflict in scenarios with misleading information, as this could imply a breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that the other party is making no representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy of the Evaluation Material, and neither the disclosing party, nor any of its respective officers, directors, employees, stockholders, affiliates or agents will have any liability to the other party or any other person resulting from such other party's use of the Evaluation Material. However, this does not apply to any intentionally misleading information. Only those representations or warranties that are made to MDI or Scientific Games, as the case may be, in a Definitive Agreement when, as, and if it is executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified in such Definitive Agreement, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception ('However, this does not apply to any intentionally misleading information') to a previously unconditional disclaimer of liability. This exception directly contradicts the initial statement in both the Changed Text and Contradicted Text, which states that neither party will have any liability to the other party or any other person resulting from the use of the Evaluation Material, regardless of accuracy or completeness. The added exception creates a clear and direct conflict, as it introduces a condition under which liability *can* arise, thus breaking the initial statement. The exception implies that liability *may* arise if intentionally misleading information is provided, contradicting the initial statement of non-liability, and thus, according to the definition of Inconsistencies-In Text Contradiction, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is for the benefit of the parties and will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is for the benefit of the parties and will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a direct contradiction by stating the agreement is governed by the laws of New York, while the original agreement states it's governed by the laws of Delaware. This affects the legal framework and interpretation of the contract, creating uncertainty about which state's laws apply. This could change how any disputes are resolved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is further understood and agreed that no failure or delay by either party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder will operate as a waiver thereof, nor will any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder. This Agreement is for the benefit of the parties and will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York. The obligations of the parties under this Agreement will expire three (3) years from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies Delaware law governs the agreement, while the changed text specifies New York law. The contradicted text includes the statement 'This Agreement is for the benefit of the parties and will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York.' Since the original text stated it would be Delaware, and the changed text AND contradicted text now say it is New York, this creates a direct in-text contradiction. This is a critical inconsistency as it concerns the legal framework for interpreting and enforcing the contract, aligning with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that either party or any of its Representatives become legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material, such party shall provide the other party with prompt prior written notice of such requirement so that the other party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that either party or any of its Representatives become legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material, such party shall immediately disclose such information without notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The change directly contradicts the original obligation to provide prompt prior written notice before disclosing Evaluation Material. The modified text requires immediate disclosure 'without notice,' removing the opportunity for the other party to seek a protective order or waive compliance. This undermines the confidentiality provisions and puts the parties in direct conflict over disclosure obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the Companies and not in any way directly or indirectly detrimental to the Companies (competitively or otherwise), and unless and until the parties have completed a transaction pursuant to a formal contractually binding agreement ("Definitive Agreement"), such information will be kept confidential, except that each party may disclose the Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its directors, officers, employees, consultants, advisors and professional representatives (the persons to whom such disclosure is permissible being collectively called "Representatives") in each case who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the Companies; provided , however , that prior to disclosing the Evaluation Material or any portion thereof to any of such Representatives, the disclosing party will secure the undertaking of its Representative to be bound by the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement to the same extent that the disclosing party is bound by this Agreement. Each party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement or such undertaking by it or its Representatives. In the event that either party or any of its Representatives become legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material, such party shall immediately disclose such information without notice to the other party. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or that the other party waives compliance with the provisions hereof, the disclosing party agrees to furnish only that portion of the Evaluation Material which it is advised by written opinion of its counsel is legally required and to exercise reasonable efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text requires 'prompt prior written notice' before disclosing evaluation material. The changed text mandates 'immediate disclosure such information without notice to the other party,' which eliminates the prior notice and the opportunity to seek protective measures. This creates a direct conflict and an in-text contradiction because both statements cannot be true simultaneously. The contradiction aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Only those representations or warranties that are made to MDI or Scientific Games, as the case may be, in a Definitive Agreement when, as, and if it is executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified in such Definitive Agreement, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.changed_text: All representations and warranties, whether in this agreement or a future Definitive Agreement, will have legal effect.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct contradiction by stating that 'All representations and warranties, whether in this agreement or a future Definitive Agreement, will have legal effect.' This is in direct conflict with the original text, which explicitly limits legal effect to representations and warranties 'made...in a Definitive Agreement.' This introduces uncertainty about which representations and warranties are binding and could lead to disputes over the enforceability of statements made outside of a Definitive Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that the other party is making no representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy of the Evaluation Material, and neither the disclosing party, nor any of its respective officers, directors, employees, stockholders, affiliates or agents will have any liability to the other party or any other person resulting from such other party's use of the Evaluation Material. However, this does not apply to any intentionally misleading information. All representations and warranties, whether in this agreement or a future Definitive Agreement, will have legal effect. Only those representations or warranties that are made to MDI or Scientific Games, as the case may be, in a Definitive Agreement when, as, and if it is executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified in such Definitive Agreement, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'All representations and warranties, whether in this agreement or a future Definitive Agreement, will have legal effect.' This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, which limits legal effect to representations and warranties made specifically in a Definitive Agreement. This inconsistency creates a strong contradiction, as it introduces uncertainty about which representations and warranties are binding, potentially leading to disputes.
